entries.title: AI Cold Email Subject Variant Generator
entries.notes: Buildable in 2-3 weeks using OpenAI's API and a small prompt-engine prompt pack per persona. The wave of AI-powered sales tooling (Smartlead, Instantly) created a pipeline gap: nothing scores subject lines pre-send. Clear freemium hook (5 free variants/month) before $19/mo seat. Competitor gap: existing tools optimize send time, not the line itself.
entries.pain: Sales reps waste hours A/B testing subject lines with no signal on which phrasing actually drives replies.

entries.title: Local LLM Page Q&A Sidebar
entries.notes: Pairs with WebLLM/transformers.js to run a 1-3B param model in a sidebar; zero data leaves the browser. Buildable in 3-4 weeks since the heavy lifting is inference plumbing. Why now: Chrome's prompt API and local model story matured in 2026. Competitor gap: Merlin/Harvey all hit server APIs; nobody owns the local-first AI wrapper category.
entries.pain: Users want to query any webpage with AI but cloud tools leak their reading habits and clipboard contents to vendors.

entries.title: Supabase RLS Policy Coverage Heatmap
entries.notes: Detects a Supabase project from cookies/JWT and walks the public REST schema to flag tables without RLS or with policy gaps. Buildable in 3 weeks using supabase-js in the page context. Why now: Supabase crossed 5M projects and security incidents are surfacing. Competitor gap: Supabase linter is server-side and noisy; this is a focused browser-side auditor.
entries.pain: Teams ship Supabase projects to staging not knowing which tables lack row-level security policies until an audit finds exposed rows.

entries.title: Conference CFP Topic Fit Scorer
entries.notes: Scrapes a CFP's past accepted talks (or uses user-pasted archive), clusters topics, and scores a draft abstract's fit. 2-3 weeks of scraping + embeddings. Why now: CFP platforms (Sessionize, Pretalx) expose data but offer no AI scoring. Niche but loyal dev audience; donation + conference sponsor pitch is the play.
entries.pain: Developers miss speaking deadlines because assessing whether their topic fits a CFP's historical vibe takes longer than drafting the talk.

entries.title: LinkedIn Promotion Velocity Heatmap
entries.notes: Aggregates LinkedIn job-posting cadence and promotion-tagged profile updates per company into a 12-week velocity score surfaced as a Sales Navigator overlay. 3-4 weeks of scraping + heuristics. Why now: Clay and Apollo lead-gen tools exploded but none focus on org-chart momentum signals. Per-seat $39/mo for SDR teams.
entries.pain: AEs burn prospecting cycles on accounts showing no recent hiring momentum in the roles their product actually targets.

entries.title: Browser Cookie Scope Origin Mapper
entries.notes: Parses Set-Cookie attributes on every response and renders an interactive graph of cookie scope across origins, highlighting SameSite=None and cross-domain reads. 2 weeks of pure DevTools work. Why now: cookie phaseout in Chrome makes scope metadata even more opaque. Differentiator from consent tools: visual-first, no regulatory framing.
entries.pain: Users can't tell which third-party domains can read cookies set on the site they're currently visiting.

entries.title: Cold Email Domain Reputation Pulse
entries.notes: Detects the user's outbound sending domain from mailto: links and inbox headers, then pings reputation APIs (Google Postmaster, MXToolbox, Spamhaus) to surface a live health badge in Gmail/Outlook. 2-3 weeks. Why now: Gmail/Yahoo 2024 sender rules raised the stakes; SDR teams need pre-flight checks. Competitor gap: existing tools are dashboards, not in-inbox nudges.
entries.pain: Outbound teams don't learn their sending domain reputation has tanked until deliverability collapses and pipeline dries up.

entries.title: Web3 Wallet Connection Risk Auditor
entries.notes: Hooks window.ethereum and WalletConnect request events, decodes ERC-7715 permissions and eth_sign typed-data payloads, then grades risk against a signature abuse database. 3-4 weeks. Why now: wallet drainers cost users $1B+ in 2025; consumers want a Slither-for-wallets layer. Freemium with pro scam-feed sync.
entries.pain: Users blindly approve wallet connection requests without seeing which on-chain permissions and signatures the dApp is actually asking for.

entries.title: Job Description AI Exposure Index
entries.notes: Scores any LinkedIn/Indeed posting on a 0-100 AI exposure scale using task taxonomy + LLM zero-shot classification, with a side-panel rationale. 2 weeks of model work plus a small labeled set. Why now: AI job displacement anxiety is the dominant career story of 2026. Tip jar at $3/mo plus referral to career coaches.
entries.pain: Job seekers can't quantify how AI-vulnerable a posting's role is before investing hours tailoring an application.

entries.title: AI Response Hallucination Spotter
entries.notes: Runs a side panel over ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini; cross-references claims against search snippets and highlights unsupported sentences in real time. 4-5 weeks using SerpApi + cheap classifiers. Why now: enterprise AI policies in 2026 mandate provenance metadata on AI-generated copy. Clear freemium wedge (10 checks/day) before $15/mo.
entries.pain: Users paste AI outputs into client work and emails without knowing which factual claims are unsourced or contradicted.
